description: A styled screenshot of a Fortune news article on a white background. The bold black serif headline reads 'Meet a 23-year-old electrician who was a 'good student' but skipped college to join Gen Z's blue-collar revolution. He makes 6 figures'. Below: byline 'BY NICK LICHTENBERG' and a date 'September 14, 2025 at 9:00 AM EDT', with a circular share icon at right. The lower half is a photograph of a smiling young man (long brown hair) in a black work shirt, arms crossed, wearing a black-and-white 'PMR' ball cap and standing in front of a red service truck/cabinet emblazoned with the 'PMR' logo and a partial phone number; shelving with parts is visible at left. This is a curated press clipping promoting the skilled-trades-over-college narrative central to DOL's 'Make America Skilled Again' campaign. The propaganda technique is third-party media validation plus an aspirational success story aimed at young people. The tweet reinforces it: 'Jacob Palmer decided to go straight into the Skilled Trades after high school instead of attending college. He now makes SIX FIGURES.' Target audience: Gen Z / young workers and parents.
notable_text: Meet a 23-year-old electrician who was a 'good student' but skipped college to join Gen Z's blue-collar revolution. He makes 6 figures
